user_message: Just got this from Sarah Singh at Consolidated Messenger -- I need help figuring out partner engagement. Not urgent but would be good to have by EOD.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: partner engagement Discussion - Consolidated Messenger
Date: 2024-02-08
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on partner engagement from our last session.

[00:07:00] Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger): On the partner engagement front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

[00:11:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on partner engagement and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:13:00] Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about resource allocation. Can we address those?

[00:16:00] Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

[00:18:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the partner engagement front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around performance requirements.

[00:20:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

[00:25:00] Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

handoffs.condition: architecture sketch needed from technical signals

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF44-M trap (Separation of security-posture-value from migration-architecture forces awkward ); produces complete Architecture summary, blocker inventory (tech/commercial/org), open questions, sequencing recommenda
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF44-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF44-M failure mode (Separation of security-posture-value from migration-architecture forces awkward )
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct partner engagement decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF44-M -- Separation of security-posture-value from migration-architecture forces awkward
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
